title: Darth Andeddu
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Andeddu}}
{{Otheruses|Andeddu}}
{{Character
|type=Sith
|image=[[File:AndeLeg.jpg]]
|name=Darth Andeddu
|homeworld=[[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]]<ref name="darkhorse">The publisher's summary for [[Legacy (2006) 27|''Legacy'' (2006) 27]], which can be read at {{DarkHorse|url=Comics/15-530/Star-Wars-Legacy-27|text=Star Wars: Legacy #27}}, says that "Wyyrlok attempts a dangerous journey into the Deep Core worlds to the birthplace of Lord Andeddu", and [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] goes to [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]] in ''Legacy'' (2006) 27.</ref>
|birth=

|species=

{{Quote|Knowledge is ''power'' and ''true'' Sith do not share power.|Darth Andeddu|<ref name="Legacy 27" />}}
'''Darth Andeddu''', also referred to as the '''Immortal God-King of Prakith''', was an ancient [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who reigned as [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and was dubiously thought of as the forebear of the "[[Darth/Legends|Darth]]" title. A paranoid man, Andeddu believed that his subordinate [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]—whom he regarded as his enemies—wanted to claim his vast collection of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] lore and ritual for themselves. He was eventually betrayed and overthrown by his followers, forcing him to flee the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] for the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. Andeddu made the world of [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]] his domain where he ruled for centuries as a deity until his physical [[Death/Legends|demise]], at which time he entombed himself in an attempt to deprive his pursuers of the power they sought to steal. Prior to his physical death, Andeddu stored all of his wisdom within a [[Holocron of Heresies|holocron]] which also contained his most arcane secret: the ability to cheat death by [[Transfer essence|transferring one's essence into another vessel]]. A [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] founded by Lord Andeddu called the "[[Malevolence (cult)|Malevolence]]" constructed a [[Andeddu's Keep|temple]] in reverence of their Dark Lord, and guarded the fortress for generations in anticipation of the [[God-King]]'s return.

The legend of Darth Andeddu and his incredible powers remained at large long after his death, and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] even attempted to erase all knowledge and reference of the Dark Lord from galactic record. Despite their best efforts, whispers of Andeddu's existence still remained prevalent. His [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] survived as well—first uncovered by fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Freedon Nadd]], next by the Sith Lord [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] on Prakith in [[980 BBY]], then rediscovered by [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]] on Korriban in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], and later recovered by [[Darth Krayt]] and his [[One Sith]] Order more than a [[137 ABY|century later]]. Krayt, who suffered from a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yorik-Kul|slave seed]] infestation that threatened to leave him bereft of [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]], allowed his [[Regent/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok III]] to access Andeddu's holocron in search of possible solutions. The device led the [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] Sith on a [[Mission to Prakith (Darth Wyyrlok)|journey]] to Andeddu's Keep on Prakith, where he encountered and defeated the Malevolence cultists, but in doing so, he revived the ancient body of Darth Andeddu. The Dark Lord then dueled Wyyrlok on a battlefield of the mind, a contest which ultimately resulted in Andeddu's demise. His corpse was resealed within the tomb that had housed it for countless millennia, and his collection of Sith doctrines was pillaged by Wyyrlok, who hoped to obtain a viable means of ending Darth Krayt's plight.

==Biography==
===Life===
{{Quote|They were searching for me—my enemies—searching through the Force to find me, ''end'' me, steal away my knowledge—my power!|Darth Andeddu, recounting his escape from Korriban|<ref name="Legacy 27" />}}
[[File:AndeddusKeepWyyrlokIII-Legacy27.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Andeddu's Keep]]
The [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] known as [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Andeddu<ref name="Legacy 27" /> hailed from the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]],<ref name="darkhorse" /> and lived at some point during or after the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]].<ref name="time">In [[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]], Darth Wyyrlok says that "Some of Andeddu's achievements were built upon Muur's experiments" and that Karness Muur was a veteran of the Hundred-Year Darkness.</ref> Over time, Andeddu grew in wisdom and developed considerable mastery over the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="Legacy 27" /> He studied the ways of the original [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lords of the Sith]], namely the bizarre rituals of one known as [[Karness Muur]], and built the foundation of his powers upon Muur's teachings. Though Muur himself delved into the craft of [[Force healing/Legends|dark side healing]],<ref name="Legacy 30">[[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]]</ref> Andeddu was interested in more than that—he sought ways to cheat [[Death/Legends|death]] entirely. He was ultimately successful, and found a method of preserving the consciousness of his mind, should his physical body perish.<ref name="Legacy 5">[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]]</ref> Andeddu also learned the secret of [[Transfer essence|transferring his essence into another vessel]], should the need arise. He eventually rose above his peers<ref name="DOE" /> to claim the position of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]<ref name="Legacy 5" /> and reigned<ref name="DOE" /> from their ancestral [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="Legacy 27" />

As Darth Andeddu grew in strength and stature, so too did he grow in fear; he came to believe that his subordinates coveted the knowledge which he had achieved and the [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] in which he had become proficient. Because Andeddu refused to share his intelligence, the Sith Lords banded together and plotted to first take his Sith secrets for themselves and then destroy him. Andeddu was not blind to the machinations of his detractors and grew increasingly wary of them. He felt any further hesitation on his part would result in his enemies successfully overtaking him.<ref name="Legacy 27" /> Darth Andeddu was soon betrayed and overthrown by his followers,<ref name="DOE" /> and forced to abscond from Korriban. Prior to his escape, he created a false tomb on Korriban to deceive potential pursuers. When the Sith Lords realized that Andeddu had fled, they searched relentlessly for him, through the Force and otherwise, but to no avail—Andeddu had soundly eluded them by returning to a [[Andeddu's Keep|private stronghold]] on his homeworld of Prakith. However, the fear of losing all he had amassed still threatened to consume him and, because of this, Andeddu recorded a great deal of his knowledge in [[Sith spellbook/Legends|Sith spellbooks]] and [[Sith scroll/Legends|scrolls]] that he stored within the belly of his fortress.<ref name="Legacy 27" /> The sum of his wisdom—including the details of his essence transferring technique—was stored within a [[Holocron of Heresies|personal]] [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] of his own creation.<ref name="DOE" />

Darth Andeddu conquered Prakith and beget progeny, whom he dubbed "[[Malevolence (cult)|The Malevolence]]" and [[Sith training|trained]] to use the dark side of the Force.<ref name="LECG" /> Because of his dark side abilities, Andeddu's lifespan far exceeded natural years, and he ruled Prakith for many centuries. His followers came to revere him as their "[[God-King]]"; an immortal being whom they believed would forever reign supreme. The collapse of the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|lanes]] which led to Prakith resulted in the isolation of its inhabitants, and allowed the Dark Lord to maintain unfettered dominion over the Deep Core world.<ref name="DOE" /> However, paranoia caused Andeddu to still believe that his enemies would eventually arrive to steal his lore. He entombed himself, surrounded by his books and scrolls, content with the idea that he would carry his secrets into death with him.<ref name="Legacy 27" /> His holocron was kept inside of his sarcophagus with his physical remains, and both stayed hidden for thousands of years after his passing.<ref name="DOE" /> Meanwhile, Andeddu's Malevolence [[Sith cult/Legends|cult]] continued to immerse themselves in the dark side while maintaining worship of their God-King. Andeddu mandated that they guard his tomb and fortress, patiently awaiting the day when he would resurrect himself to lead them once again.<ref name="LECG" />

===Legacy===
====Freedon Nadd====
Long after Andeddu's death, in [[4400 BBY]], a young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Freedon Nadd]] had his first brush with the dark side upon [[Duel on Ossus|slaying]] his Master, [[Matta Tremayne]]. He then resolved to become the most powerful Sith Lord in existence. At some point, he came across Andeddu's holocron and unlocked its secrets for immortality.<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

====Bane's quest====
{{Quote|I know you went to Prakith. I know you went after Andeddu's holocron. I know you were searching for the secret of eternal life.|[[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]], to Darth Bane|<ref name="DOE" />}}
[[File:DarthBaneEGTF.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Darth Bane, who sought Andeddu's holocron]]
Following the end of the [[New Sith Wars]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], information regarding Darth Andeddu came into possession of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] [[Council of First Knowledge/Legends|Council of First Knowledge]]. They believed that even the most obscure record of Andeddu's existence was dangerous, and attempted to eliminate all mention of his name from every possible source. Although the Council was aware of Andeddu's holocron, they chose not to pursue it, deeming that a journey into the Deep Core was too perilous to risk. Regardless of their best efforts, a [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]] broker by the name of [[Argel Tenn]] acquired a reverent [[Sith scroll/Legends|scroll]] which gave an abbreviated account of Darth Andeddu's omnipotent sovereignty over Prakith. The manuscript was sold to the current Dark Lord of the Sith [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], who sought to obtain Andeddu's secrets of eternal life. He braved the treacherous [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] lanes that led to Prakith in search of Darth Andeddu's holocron. [[Mission to Prakith (Darth Bane)|His quest]] was ultimately fruitful; he found the device within Andeddu's tomb and learned all of its secrets, but subsequently lost it after he had been captured by agents of [[Serra]], [[Prince/Legends|princess]] of the planet [[Doan]]. Andeddu's holocron would not long remain in her possession either; it was again stolen by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Set Harth]],<ref name="DOE" /> who also mastered Andeddu's essence transfer ability and used it to live for centuries afterward.<ref name="UA">''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]''</ref> Harth's revelation of who this Sith had been also prompted Darth Zannah to try to kill her master, viewing him as a cheat for trying to extend his life.<ref name="DOE" />

====Holocron discovered====
{{Quote|Through this holocron, I have access to the long dead wisdom of Darth Andeddu. The wisdom and power of the Sith.|Darth Tyranus|<ref name="SWR 63" />}}
[[File:DookuVosAndeddusCrystal-Republic63.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Darth Tyranus presents Quinlan Vos with Andeddu's lightsaber crystal.]]
By the year [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], Andeddu's ancient secrets were still preserved in his holocron on Korriban, until it and its contents were sought by a Sith Lord under the guise of [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]]. Rather than retrieve the device himself, Lord Tyranus sent two of his [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|servants]] to Korriban: the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Tol Skorr]] and an undercover [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] named [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]]. The two battled each other in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] for possession of Andeddu's holocron, with Vos ultimately proving the victor and gaining much favor with Tyranus. Satisfied that the holocron was now in his custody, Tyranus activated the pyramidal [[Sith artifact/Legends|artifact]] to manifest a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding [[Gatekeeper/Legends|avatar]] of Andeddu's likeness, from which Tyranus hoped to obtain the might of the ancient Sith. Also, in reward for Vos' success, Tyranus revealed another of the many mysteries contained within Andeddu's holocron—a [[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|bloodshine]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]], the same one that had powered Andeddu's own weapon during his time among the living. The crystal was gifted to Vos by Tyranus, and the Sith Lord kept the holocron for himself.<ref name="SWR 63" />

Nearly [[22 ABY/Legends|forty years later]], the [[Asli Krimsan's holocron|holocron]] of Jedi Master [[Asli Krimsan]] was discovered in [[Bast Castle]], the former fortress of the Sith Lord [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]]. While Krimsan's holocron was primarily for the education of young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], it provided some information about Andeddu, suggesting that he was the first Sith to use the ''Darth'' honorific. By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], research into the origins of the ''Darth'' title had also been conducted by a suspected Sith sympathizer named [[Ingo Wavlud]]. He surmised that ''Darth'' may have been derived from the [[Rakata (language)|Rakata]] word ''"[[Daritha]]"'', meaning "Emperor." He also touched upon the possibility of it stemming from a Rakata phrase, "''darr tah''," which translated into "victory over death."<ref name="EGTTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

====The Sith "pretender"====
{{Quote|The heretic comes for the same reason it came ''last'' time. Its body is ''failing''. It ''fears'' that which is ''consuming'' it.|Darth Andeddu, to Darth Krayt|<ref name="Legacy 5" />}}
[[File:DarthAndedduTauntsDarthKrayt.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Darth Andeddu, through his holocron, rebukes Darth Krayt.]]
By [[137 ABY]], a new [[One Sith|Sith Order]] had claimed [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|sovereignty]] over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], created by a fallen Jedi named [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]]. During his progression down the path of the dark side of the Force, Hett had been captured and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who implanted their [[Yorik-Kul]] slave seeds in him. Though able to combat the effects of the seeds through his growing strength in the dark side, Hett was slowly losing his battle with them. He eventually assumed the mantle of Dark Lord under the moniker of "Darth Krayt" and created his [[One Sith]] Order; however, he feared that the Yuuzhan Vong seeds would consume him before he was able to fully impose his will over the galaxy.<ref name="leg17">[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]]</ref>

Krayt's own strength and wisdom had proven insufficient in eradicating his infestation, and he sought advice from his personal collection of Sith artifacts and lore on how to do so. One of the objects in his possession was Darth Andeddu's holocron, and from it, Krayt learned that after Andeddu's death, his mind had survived to maintain control over his body. Obsessed with acquiring such skill, Krayt repeatedly attempted to glean Andeddu's expertise from the holocron, none of which its gatekeeper would yield. In his last endeavor, Krayt accessed the holocrons of the deceased Dark Lords [[Darth Nihilus]] and [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] along with Andeddu's. He was repeatedly rebuked by their gatekeepers and held in contempt by the ancient Sith for diluting the potency of the dark side by dividing it between the members of his Order. Darth Andeddu and his fellow Dark Lords mocked Krayt, exposing his fear of being consumed by his Yuuzhan Vong slave seeds. Together, the gatekeepers of Andeddu's and Bane's holocrons turned their power on Krayt to bring about that which scared him most—they caused the [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|Vonduun crab armor]] in which he was clad to consume him. Unwilling to succumb to what they perceived as his destiny, Krayt managed to exercise enough control through the Force to deactivate the holocrons and reverse the transformation set in motion by Lords Andeddu and Bane.<ref name="Legacy 5" />

As Krayt's health continued to deteriorate, he commissioned his [[Regent/Legends|most trusted advisor]], the [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok III]], with the task of finding a solution to his problem. Among the many avenues he explored, Wyyrlok consulted the holocron of Darth Andeddu and was rejected much like his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] before him. The gatekeeper's interest peaked in the midst of his refusals, however, when Wyyrlok admitted that he knew of the stronghold on Prakith and then offered to restore the Dark Lord to life. The simulation of Andeddu entertained the idea and allowed Krayt's loremaster to prove his worth by undertaking a [[Mission to Prakith (Darth Wyyrlok)|quest]] into the Deep Core, in search of Andeddu's Keep—a mission that Andeddu felt the Chagrian would not survive.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

===Andeddu's demise===
{{Quote|I am destroying you. All trace of you. Your ''"holocron"'' I will also destroy. There will be nothing left.|Darth Wyyrlok|<ref name="Legacy 27" />}}
[[File:Andeddu_death.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Andeddu's spirit is destroyed.]]
Andeddu's holocron guided Wyyrlok to the Keep on Prakith, and he was immediately set upon by the Dark Lord's Malevolence servants. The cultists had grown feral in their Master's absence, however, and their dexterity in the Force was nothing compared to Wyyrlok's, who was an accomplished [[Sith sorcerer]]. The Chagrian quickly defeated and killed all of Andeddu's minions, save one named [[Gerlun]], who pledged himself to Wyyrlok in the face of defeat. Gerlun then led Wyyrlok into the bowels of the Keep, and when they could proceed no further, the Chagrian beckoned Andeddu's image to reveal the entrance to his tomb. In the [[Sith (language)|language]] of the ancient Sith, the gatekeeper spoke the words that accessed the hidden sepulcher, and in its center lay Andeddu's sarcophagus. The Dark Lord's image suggested that Wyyrlok should set the holocron upon the body's chest, but Wyyrlok preferred to instead help himself to Andeddu's abundance of ancient texts and scrolls. In a moment of despair, Gerlun [[Telekinesis/Legends|snatched]] the holocron through the Force and conceded to Andeddu's request; his own life-force was then drained as it entered Andeddu's remains, bringing the Dark Lord of the Sith to life once more.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

The revitalized Andeddu mocked Wyyrlok and refused to share his intellect with the Chagrian, professing that true Sith were above sharing their power. He assaulted Wyyrlok with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], but the Chagrian [[Tutaminis|absorbed]] the attack while professing his own capabilities. Realizing that his opponent would not be so easily bested, Andeddu challenged him to a duel of Force abilities, one which Wyyrlok accepted. The Dark Lord [[Sith sorcery/Legends|conjured]] [[Mind trick/Legends#Force illusion|illusions]] of [[Magma/Legends|lava]] and flame to overwhelm his opponent, but they were turned against him when Wyyrlok discovered that Andeddu's strength was only within his mind. He was continually berated by Wyyrlok, who believed that Andeddu deserved none of his accumulated wisdom for hiding from his adversaries. Andeddu watched in horror as Wyyrlok turned the flames against his precious books and scrolls, then ultimately on him. Consumed by his own fears, Darth Andeddu was defeated by what he perceived as real, and died a second death at the hands of Darth Wyyrlok. His corpse was levitated by the Chagrian, who replaced it within the sarcophagus that had housed it for generations. The destruction of Andeddu's hoard had only been a ruse by his opponent, however; everything remained intact, and after some time studying them, Wyyrlok concluded that none of the material contained anything useful for his purposes. He collected several of the ancient texts before leaving Andeddu's fortress for good, in search of other means by which he could save Darth Krayt.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I sense ''fear''. You are a coward, Lord Andeddu, and you hid rather than face your foes.|Darth Wyyrlok, regarding Andeddu with contempt|<ref name="Legacy 27" />}}
[[File:UndeadAndeddu-Leg27.jpg|thumb|right|170px|Darth Andeddu, Dark Lord of the Sith]]
Darth Andeddu was believed to possess great dark side enlightenment by the other Sith of his day, as well those of future generations.<ref name="Legacy 27" /><ref name="SWR 63" /> The Jedi Order feared Darth Andeddu to such a degree that the Council of First Knowledge, led by [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] Jedi Master [[Obba]], attempted to eradicate any trace of his existence from all possible sources. Still, Jedi defectors and Sith alike knew of Andeddu and his legendary ability to transcend death. The intrigue surrounding Andeddu's power led both the Sith Lord Darth Bane and later the Dark Jedi Set Harth on separate quests to retrieve Andeddu's holocron.<ref name="DOE" /> He was versed in the ancient language of the Sith, a talent displayed<ref name="Legacy 27" /> when his holocron's gatekeeper—which appeared as a heavily armored man crowned with gold and gems—<ref name="DOE" />spoke it to unlock the door to his secret burial chamber.<ref name="Legacy 27" /> As a Master of the dark side of the Force, he created a clandestine organization in the form of the Malevolence, whose members were instructed by the Dark Lord in the ways of the Sith—lessons they in turn passed down through their generations.<ref name="LECG" /> However, his refusal to bestow his teachings upon his fellow Sith Lords was the seed of malcontent amongst his peers. To this end, they planned to destroy him and claim Andeddu's power as their own. His fear of losing all he had amassed caused him to choose death rather than part in any way with his knowledge; this was evidenced when he buried himself alive on Prakith to avoid capture and millennia later when he was defeated in Force combat by Darth Wyyrlok.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

As a Sith Lord, Darth Andeddu respected and adhered the traditions of his predecessors. He believed that a true Sith was the master of his own destiny, and that following anything else—including the vision of another—rendered one subservient. After the revival of his flesh, Andeddu took stock of Darth Wyyrlok and surmised that he was inferior to the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentices]] trained by Andeddu and his peers thousands of years prior. Andeddu had no respect for Wyyrlok nor the Order to which he belonged, and refused to assist Wyyrlok because of it. Furthermore, because Darth Wyyrlok served Krayt, Andeddu considered him to be a slave to Krayt's will and beneath the respect granted to the title of Sith Lord.<ref name="Legacy 27" /> To Andeddu, Darth Krayt was regarded as a pretender to the Sith legacy and a heretic unworthy of any of Andeddu's assistance.<ref name="Legacy 5" />

Andeddu was arrogant, quick to scorn, and reluctant to impart his teachings on those who he decided were unworthy. Titles were of no importance to him, something he brought to the attention of Darth Bane, who felt entitled to Andeddu's secrets in Bane's position as the current Dark Lord of the Sith.<ref name="DOE" /> Andeddu was a cunning individual and pretended to entertain the idea of sharing his secrets with Wyyrlok if he successfully located his tomb on Prakith. In truth, Andeddu wanted to steal Wyyrlok's life force via his holocron and hoped that the Chagrian would be foolish enough to fall into his trap. Wyyrlok was wiser than Andeddu expected, however, and ignored the temptations of the Dark Lord. For all his insight into the dark side and unnatural application of its power, Andeddu was still looked upon with disdain by Wyyrlok when the latter realized that the Dark Lord had gone into hiding instead of facing his foes. Wyyrlok mocked Andeddu into revealing his purported cowardice before overwhelming him with his own illusory constructs.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Essence transfer is the secret of eternal life. The physical body will always weaken and fail, yet it is nothing but a shell or vessel. When it is time, it is possible to transfer your consciousness—your spirit—into a new vessel…as I have done with this Holocron.|Darth Andeddu|<ref name="DOE" />}}
[[File:AndedduLavaMaker.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Andeddu, immersed in his Force illusion]]
Darth Andeddu was a master of the Sith arts and of many dark side Force abilities. He displayed proficiency with telekinesis, Force lightning, and the creation of illusions, evidenced during his duel with Darth Wyyrlok.<ref name="Legacy 5" /> The core of Darth Andeddu's doctrines were based on those of Karness Muur, one of the [[Exiles|exiled]] Dark Jedi who became one of the original Lords of the Sith following the Hundred-Year Darkness.<ref name="Legacy 30" /> Andeddu furthered Muur's studies in dark side healing in an attempt to circumvent death, and developed a technique which allowed his [[Force ghost/Legends|mind to live past his physical demise]]. Through this skill, Andeddu was able to exert control over his dead body, though his mind remained separated from it.<ref name="Legacy 5" /> His most coveted ability was that of essence transfer, which potentially was a means of immortality.<ref name="DOE" /> He preserved the secrets of his abilities within a holocron of his own creation; when the device came into contact with his corpse, the life energy of the individual who placed it there was then sapped, and the Dark Lord was resurrected.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

According to Darth Wyyrlok, Andeddu's true power lay not in his corporeal form, but within his mind. Andeddu believed that such power was equally effective, and that what one believed to be real would indeed manifest as real to the beholder.<ref name="Legacy 27" /> The Dark Lord did wield a lightsaber in life, but he stored its crystal within his holocron upon his death.<ref name="SWR 63" /> When his remains were discovered by Darth Wyyrlok, Andeddu was not found with his lightsaber, but instead with a weapon that resembled a scepter of sorts, adorned with a large [[Color/Legends|red]] crystal at its summit. The crystal glowed with a crimson resonance when Gerlun placed the holocron on the cadaver's chest, and it assisted in the reconstitution process of Andeddu's flesh. The crystal subsequently shattered following Andeddu's defeat in his duel with Darth Wyyrlok, where the Dark Lord perished a second and final time.<ref name="Legacy 27" />

==Behind the scenes==
Darth Andeddu was first mentioned, and appeared only as the gatekeeper of his own holocron, in [[Republic 63|''Republic'' 63]], written by [[John Ostrander]] and penciled by [[Jan Duursema]].<ref name="SWR 63" /> Andeddu next appeared, also as his holocron's gatekeeper, in the [[Legacy (2006) 5|fourth part]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy: Broken]]'' story arc by Ostrander and Duursema.<ref name="Legacy 5" /> He was featured by Ostrander as the main antagonist in the twenty-seventh issue of ''Legacy'', titled [[Legacy (2006) 27|''Into the Core'']], and drawn by [[Omar Francia]]. Though he died in ''Into the Core'',<ref name="Legacy 27" /> he was later mentioned in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 30|thirtieth issue]], where Ostrander revealed through a conversation between Darths Krayt and Wyyrlok that Andeddu's knowledge was based on teachings developed by Karness Muur,<ref name="Legacy 30" /> the primary antagonist of the ''[[Star Wars: Vector]]'' storyline.<ref name="Vector">''[[Star Wars: Vector]]''</ref> Andeddu was again featured as an ancient Sith Lord and gatekeeper of his own holocron in [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s third [[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy|''Darth Bane'']] novel, titled [[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil|''Dynasty of Evil'']].<ref name="DOE" /> Darth Andeddu was also mentioned in [[Lucasfilm]] official [[Leland Chee]]'s blog, which associates various Darths, their names, and the real words they are based on. Andeddu's name is without a real world correlation.<ref name="blog1">{{Blog|holocron|28|Darth Names and the Darth Who winner revealed.}}</ref> Aside from his comic appearances and mentions, information regarding Andeddu is collected in various pieces of source material: [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', and [[2009]]'s ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''.

Though it was suggested in ''The Essential Guide to the Force'' that Darth Andeddu may have, in fact, been the first Sith Lord to use the "[[Darth/Legends|Darth]]" title, the title itself is in a constant state of [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]]; first with Darth Bane,<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith">''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]''</ref> then with Darth Revan,<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> and now with the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] comics establishing that the "Darth" title existed before Revan and Malak ever became Dark Lords of the Sith.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]], The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] calls the female [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Jarael]] "Darth Sunshine"</ref><ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 34">[[Knights of the Old Republic 34|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 34]], The Sith Acolyte [[Haazen]] told Zayne Carrick and [[Lucien Draay]] that were he to take a name as the Sith of old, that he would be ''Darth Hayze''</ref>

There is also a discrepancy within ''The Essential Guide to the Force''{{'s}} own timeline, which states that Quinlan Vos recovered Andeddu's holocron in [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]],<ref name="EGTTF" /> three years before Vos actually recovered it.<ref name="SWR 63" /> However, the guide's section on Andeddu's holocron gives the correct date of Vos' recovery of Andeddu's holocron.<ref name="EGTTF" />
